title: Function pointers should not be converted to any other type
description: Function pointers should not be converted to any other type. Conversion of a function pointer to a different type of pointer results in undefined behaviour. This means, for example, that a pointer to a function cannot be converted to a pointer to a different type of function.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Function pointers should not be converted to incompatible types.
